finding: `taskflow close --historical-batch` writes per-task closure bundles but does not stage the referenced historical batch envelope, leaving `.atm/history/evidence/historical-batches/<batch>.json` untracked while closure packets reference it.
expectedBehavior: The close commit bundle should include the referenced historical batch envelope, or create a governed batch-envelope commit before task-level closure packets can reference it.
evidenceOrRepro: After closing `TASK-CID-0103`, `git status --short` still showed `?? .atm/history/evidence/historical-batches/hist-batch-2026-06-16T01-40-43-634Z.json`; closure output referenced that same batch path as the source of the slice. Fast repair `5f1a4548` added envelope staging and dogfood dry-runs for `TASK-CID-0105` / `TASK-CID-0107` showed the batch envelope in `governedCommitBundle.targetRepo.stageFiles`.
